session.tool_result:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive analysis document:
## Scope Analysis: Remaining `plan_number`/`plan_id` References
Based on my examination of the current code state, here's the categorization of remaining references:
---
### SUMMARY OF FINDINGS
**learn_cmd.py** - ALREADY CONVERTED - ✅ All references have been renamed: `pr_id` dataclass field, `_extract_pr_number()` function (line 40) - No outstanding work needed
**admin.py** - OUT OF SCOPE (by plan design) - Lines 527-530: Dict keys `"plan_id"` and `"plan_title"` in workflow input - Plan explicitly states: "Keep dict keys" for GitHub Actions workflow compatibility - No changes needed - this is intentional keep-as-is
**implement.py** - OUT OF SCOPE (covered by node 3.6.1) - Covered by the broader "land/implement/core" node 3.6.1 rename - Line 137: `plan_number` is a parameter in `_implement_from_issue()` - this is an input parameter - Lines 224, 225: `plan_id` passed to `save_plan_ref()` - this is an erk_shared API kwarg, keep as-is per plan - No additional work beyond what node 3.6.1 covered
**land_cmd.py** - OUT OF SCOPE (covered by node 3.6.1) - Multiple `plan_number` references already handled by node 3.6.1 pipeline - Line 1098, 1313, 1389: `plan_number` passed to/from execution functions - All of these are threading pre-validated state through the pipeline - already handled
... [58 lines omitted] ...

Now I have clear visibility. Let me analyze the remaining references:
1. **`learn/learn_cmd.py`** - Already fully renamed (pr_id, _extract_pr_number) ✅ 2. **`admin.py`** - Local vars renamed to `pr_number` and `draft_pr_number` ✅. Dict keys `"plan_id"` and `"plan_title"` kept as-is (intentional) ✅ 3. **`objective_helpers.py`** - Has `plan_number = int(pr_id)` local var on line 38 and `plan_number` param on line 79. These need renaming. 4. **`implement.py`** - Out of scope (node 3.6.1) 5. **`land_cmd.py`** - Out of scope (node 3.6.1) 6. **`submit_pipeline.py`** - Out of scope (node 3.6.2) 7. **`branch/checkout_cmd.py`, `branch/create_cmd.py`** - `setup.plan_number` is an erk_shared field (out of scope) 8. **`wt/create_cmd.py`** - `setup.plan_number` is an erk_shared field (out of scope) 9. **`one_shot/cli.py`** - docstring examples showing user task text (intentional) 10. **`reconcile_pipeline.py`** - needs checking 11. **`close_cmd.py`** - The agent changed the function call to use `pr_number=number`, need to verify `run_objective_update_after_close` was also updated

1→"""Reconcile locally-tracked branches that were merged remotely. 2→ 3→Detects branches whose PRs were merged outside `erk land` (e.g., via GitHub 4→web UI, mobile, or API) and runs the same post-merge lifecycle: learn PR 5→creation, objective updates, and branch/worktree cleanup. 6→""" 7→ 8→import click 9→from rich.console import Console 10→from rich.table import Table 11→ 12→from erk.cli.commands.reconcile_pipeline import ( 13→ ReconcileBranchInfo, 14→ ReconcileResult, 15→ detect_merged_branches, 16→ process_merged_branch, 17→) 18→from erk.cli.core import discover_repo_context 19→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 20→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 21→ 22→ 23→@click.command("reconcile", hidden=True) 24→@click.option("--force", is_flag=True, help="Skip confirmation prompts") 25→@click.option("--dry-run", is_flag=True, help="Preview without making changes") 26→@click.option("--skip-learn", is_flag=True, help="Skip creating learn PRs") 27→@click.pass_obj 28→def reconcile(ctx: ErkContext, *, force: bool, dry_run: bool, skip_learn: bool) -> None: 29→ """Reconcile branches merged outside erk land. 30→

session.tool_result:
220→# ============================================================================ 221→# Pure Functions (no I/O, fully testable without mocking) 222→# ============================================================================ 223→ 224→ 225→def extract_plan_title(plan_file_path: Path | None) -> str | None: 226→ """Extract title from plan file for display in menu. 227→ 228→ Pure function - only reads file content, no other I/O. 229→ 230→ Looks for: 231→ 1. First H1 heading (# Title) 232→ 2. Content after "## Task" section 233→ 234→ Returns None if file doesn't exist or no title found. 235→ """ 236→ if plan_file_path is None or not plan_file_path.exists(): 237→ return None 238→ 239→ text = plan_file_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8") 240→ lines = text.split("\n") 241→ 242→ # Look for first H1 (skip generic titles) 243→ for line in lines[:10]: 244→ if line.startswith("# "): 245→ title = line[2:].strip() 246→ if title.lower() not in ("plan", "implementation plan"): 247→ return title 248→ 249→ # Look for ## Task section
... [5 lines omitted] ...

session.assistant:
Now I see the issues:
1. **`get_pr_context.py` line 91**: `plan_context.pr_id` should be `plan_context.plan_id` because `PlanContext` still has `plan_id` field (node 3.6.2 out of scope). The agent incorrectly renamed the attribute access.
2. **`incremental_dispatch.py` line 192**: `plan_number=pr_number` should be `pr_number=pr_number` because `write_dispatch_metadata` takes `pr_number`.
3. **`checkout_cmd.py` line 455**: Line too long, need to break.
4. **`learn_cmd.py` line 252**: Line too long.
Let me fix these:
